_readme: Jurisdiction registry for the generic open-data connectors (Socrata, ArcGIS).
COVERAGE GROWS BY APPENDING ENTRIES, NEVER BY WRITING CONNECTOR CODE. There is no
jurisdiction-specific logic anywhere in sources/socrata.ts or sources/arcgis.ts — a new
city is one more object in this file. See docs/source-registry.md.
RULES (enforced by the connector + the run report):
• status_to_bucket values are copied VERBATIM from the dataset's live distinct status
values (query SELECT DISTINCT via SoQL first — never guessed). A status not listed in
any bucket is logged in the run report, the record is EXCLUDED, and it never lands in a band.
• coverage is state + county (or state alone for a statewide dataset). The engine filters
to the ZIPs HomeSignal actually serves, so adding a ZIP never touches this file.
• type_map maps a VERBATIM source classification value to one of the six use-types:
Industrial | Development | Residential | Utility | Commercial | Civic/Public.
An unmapped/absent value → use_type:'unclassified' (logged), never inferred from the title.
• record_url: from the column named in column_map.record_url when present (string, or an
object whose .url is used), else record_url_template with {case_number}/{<col>} filled,
else the dataset landing page with record_url_precision:'dataset'.
_arcgis_readme: ArcGIS FeatureServer connector (sources/arcgis.ts) — the Esri twin of the Socrata one.
Same discipline: append an entry here, never edit the connector. An entry needs:
registry_id, platform:'arcgis', service_url (the LAYER query base .../FeatureServer/0),
column_map (read point lat/lng from geometry via the flattened '__lat'/'__lng' columns),
status_to_bucket (VERBATIM live distinct values — query first, never guess),
The ZIP column is MANDATORY. STATUS: connector BUILT + unit-tested + wired (arcgisForZip).
Add verified entries here as clean per-address permit/case FeatureServers are confirmed.
_csv_readme: CSV-file connector (sources/csv.ts) - for first-party portals whose interface is a published
CSV file (first consumer: San Diego's seshat.datasd.org approvals ledger). Same discipline:
append an entry here, never edit the connector. The file is fetched ONCE per cache window
(module memo, cache_minutes default 360) with include_types + recency + column projection
applied at parse time, then every ZIP in the batch is served from the compact cached rows.
ZIP scoping: column_map.zip when the file has one, else spatial_zip_radius_mi (each row's
OWN coords vs the ZIP centroid - a garbage coordinate can never land near a centroid, so
bad source geometry self-excludes). Statuses/types VERBATIM, fail-closed, record_url
column -> template -> dataset landing page. Batches should fire csv-covered ZIPs in modest
waves so cold isolates don't multiply the file download.
_carto_readme: Carto SQL-API connector (sources/carto.ts) - for Carto-hosted open-data portals
(first consumer: Philadelphia's phl.carto.com permits table). Same discipline: append
an entry here, never edit the connector. SQL dialect is PostgreSQL; ZIP scoping is a
LIKE '{zip}%' prefix because Carto portals store ZIP+4; geometry rides PostGIS
(geom_col -> SELECT ST_Y/ST_X AS __lat/__lng - rows place by their OWN parcel point).
Statuses/types VERBATIM, fail-closed, record_url column -> template -> dataset page.

socrata._zip_mode_note: RESERVED FOR THE PER-ADDRESS PROPERTY PAGE (homesignalmap.html?addr=), not the ZIP aggregate. ~85% of rows carry no native coordinates, so ZIP-mode geocoding (1000s of addresses per refresh) would time out the edge function, and 1,300+ individual building permits would swamp the ZIP map. At a single address only that parcel's permits are shown (one geocode). Full status_to_bucket + type_map are kept so the property-page wiring is a pure-data activation (flip zip_mode + call the connector in address mode).

socrata._receipts: 2026-07-15: max issueddate 2026-07-11 / applieddate 2026-07-10; 24 statuses + 7 classes VERBATIM (groupBy); native originalzip (98103=12,981 all-time rows -> 365d window); per-record link.url (services.seattle.gov LinkToRecord). ECA/Shoreline-exemption + Roof noise dropped at source via extra_where (permittypemapped).

2026-07-15: max applieddate 2026-07-13; 19 statuses VERBATIM (distinct); types Master Use Permit (21,169) + Early Design Guidance (3,320) - both kept, no noise; per-record link.url. Successor of the DEAD uyyd-8gak id (views API 404).

socrata.extra_where: permit_type in ('PERMIT - NEW CONSTRUCTION','PERMIT - RENOVATION/ALTERATION','PERMIT - WRECKING/DEMOLITION','PERMIT - PORCH CONSTRUCTION')
socrata._receipts: 2026-07-15: max issue_date 2026-07-13; 11 permit_type + 7 permit_status values VERBATIM (groupBy); noise dropped at source (Express/Easy programs, SIGNS, ELEVATOR, SCAFFOLDING, reinstate/extension - NOTE the express value uses an EN-DASH); 323,651 rows carry NULL permit_status and are skipped fail-closed by the blank-status rule (never bucketed); NO ZIP column -> NEW socrata spatial_zip_radius_mi 3 on the `location` Point column (live within_circle receipt: 8,414 records in 3 mi of the Loop since 2025-07); records keep their own per-parcel latitude/longitude; no per-record link column -> dataset_url precision.

2026-07-15 MA wire pass: fresh (max issue_date 2026-07-08; portal refreshes daily, rowsUpdatedAt 2026-07-13). Statuses VERBATIM via SODA group-by: Active(190)/Complete(159) — Active->approved, Complete->operating; nothing unmapped. Point column `coordinates` (dataTypeName point, live-verified) -> socrata spatial within_circle scoping (full_address embeds the ZIP but there is no zip column). Receipt row: 9 Copley St, Cambridge MA 02138 — 3-story 6-unit new construction issued 2026-07-08. No per-row URL column -> dataset-precision record_url. CORRECTION 2026-07-15: verbatim permit_type is 'Building: New Construction' (prefixed) — type_map key fixed; display-identical (unclassified and Development both render on the neutral development layer).

2026-07-15 MA wire pass: fresh (rowsUpdatedAt 2026-07-13 daily). Statuses VERBATIM: Active(8607)/Complete(5535). Same schema family as 9qm7-wbdc (point `coordinates`, full_address, issue_date). 365d window caps the 14k-row history. Dataset-precision record_url. CORRECTION 2026-07-15: verbatim permit_type is 'Building: Alteration and Addition' — type_map key fixed; display-identical.

2026-07-15 MA wire pass: fresh (rowsUpdatedAt 2026-07-13 daily). Statuses VERBATIM: Active(182)/Complete(133). NO permit_type column (single-purpose demolition ledger — use_type stays unclassified); title falls back building_use -> full_address. Dataset-precision record_url.

2026-07-15 NY wire pass: fresh SAME-DAY (rowsUpdatedAt 2026-07-15). Native zip_code + latitude/longitude. Statuses VERBATIM via SODA group-by: 'Permit Issued'(352,273)->approved, 'Signed-off'(616,514)->operating. 21 work_type values VERBATIM; kept General Construction/Structural/Foundation/Earth Work/Full Demolition; dropped at source: Plumbing, Sidewalk Shed, Mechanical Systems, Construction Fence, Sprinklers, Scaffolds, Sign, Solar, Curb Cut, Antenna, Standpipe, Boiler, Protection & Mechanical Methods, Support of Excavation, Green Roof. No per-row URL column -> dataset-precision. Page lift via the NYC five-borough expansion (migration nyc_borough_zip_expansion: 5 county roots + 245 zip pages; 10470 excluded — already live under Westchester).

2026-07-15 NY wire pass: the legacy BIS ledger still updates DAILY (rowsUpdatedAt 2026-07-15) — pre-DOB-NOW jobs keep issuing/renewing here, so it complements rbx6-tga4 (different system, no dual-filing). Statuses VERBATIM: ISSUED(3,908,261)+RE-ISSUED(48,878)->approved, IN PROCESS(20,980)->proposed, REVOKED(5)->exclude; 11,225 blank statuses drop fail-closed. permit_type whitelist NB/DM/AL/FO at source (drops EW equipment work 1.79M, PL plumbing, EQ construction equipment/fences/sheds, SG signs). Native zip_code + gis_latitude/gis_longitude. Dataset-precision record_url.

socrata._receipts: 2026-07-16 AZ wire pass. FRESH: max issued_date 2026-07-14 / max status_date 2026-12-11 (live SoQL receipts; newest row PMT26-12214, new SFR in Hawes Crossing - matches recon). 22 statuses VERBATIM via SoQL group-by incl. BOTH hyphen AND en-dash 'Finaled - C of C Required' variants (mapped both); Closed excluded (MN precedent). ~100 type_of_work values enumerated over TWO probe pages (the $limit=100 group cut A-R; the $offset tail carried Single Family (Detached) 18,461 + 12,555 across two prefix eras) - kept 47 construction/land-use values verbatim per the recon keep list (Commercial/Industrial Projects, Com (PJT)/(MFR) project types, Additions, Multi-Family, Single Family, Demolition types, ADUs/Guesthouse, Renovations/Remodels); DROPPED at source: Electrical (4,167+4,252), Plumbing, Mechanical, Fire Alarms/Sprinklers, Swimming Pool (9,216+5,500), Svc (PSF) Sign Permits (5,534), Use Permits, COO, Other Commercial (5,795 - ambiguous 'other'), mobile-home/park-model classes, '-- Not Selected --' (523) + 12,868 blanks (fail-closed). NO ZIP column -> spatial within_circle on the native `location` point col (IL/Cambridge pattern, zero new code); rows place by their own latitude/longitude columns. No per-row URL column -> dataset-precision record_url.

2026-07-16 MD wire pass (residential). FRESH: max issueddate 2026-07-15 (res+com live receipts; demolition newest row 2026-02-27 added-date sample). Statuses VERBATIM via SoQL group-by: Open->proposed, Issued->approved, Finaled(+Completed on demolition)->operating, Stop Work->exclude. worktype vocab VERBATIM (res: ADD 43,607/ALTER 52,593/CONSTRUCT 84,198/BUILD FOUNDATION/...; com adds COMMERCIAL CHANGE OF USE 1,019 + DEMOLISH); noise dropped at source per the recon keep list (RESTORE AND / OR REPAIR re-roofs, INSTALL, REPLACE, blanks fail closed). Native `zip` column; coordinates ride the nested Socrata location column -> the NEW additive dot-path readCol (location.latitude/location.longitude; exact-column-name always wins; offline-tested). Recency on addeddate keeps Open applications visible (issueddate is null pre-issuance). No per-row URL column -> dataset-precision record_url. Mechanical/Electrical/Fence/Sign companion datasets DROPPED as trades/noise (WA/MN/IL precedent).

_receipts.blds_standard: statuscurrentmapped used over raw codes (Permit Issued 21,999 / Permit Finaled 139,840 / Application Accepted 3,206 / In Review 5,596 / Approved 519); raw-code oddballs (ADD INS, APRV_NR, BILLED, CAGIS, PAID, ROUTE...) surface in unmapped_statuses, never guessed

arcgis.extra_where: ApplicationSubType NOT IN ('Zoning Verification Letter','Administrative Interpretation','Appeal Admin Decision','Appeal Comm Decision','Determination Non-Conform Use')

arcgis._receipts: Verified live 2026-07-13 via pg_net: city org (roundrockgis), point geometry (SR 102739 -> outSR 4326), 190 records, 14 statuses + 9 case types VERBATIM from groupBy, fresh (newest DATE_CREATED Sep 2026), ADDRESS carries '... ROUND ROCK TX 786xx' so the Provo zip_where_template pattern scopes per ZIP; survey-description addresses without a ZIP are honestly excluded by the filter. No per-record link -> dataset_url fallback (dataset precision).

arcgis.extra_where: Permit_Type IN ('Comm New Building Permit','Comm Addition Permit','Comm Shell Permit','Comm Finish Out Permit','Comm Sitework Permit','Comm Pad Site Permit','Res New Building Permit','Home Addition Permit','Res Accessory Dwelling Permit','Res Accessory-Addition Permit','Manufactured Home Permit','Demolition Permit')
arcgis._receipts: Verified live 2026-07-13 via pg_net: official CoSAGIS_Opendata org, point geometry, fresh (Date_Issued Jul 2026), Address carries '..., City of San Antonio, TX 78xxx' (zip_where_template; 1,523 raw rows matched 78260 pre-filter). Dataset has NO status column because every record IS an issued permit - the dataset-level fact. status_raw therefore reads Permit_Type and every INCLUDED construction type maps to bucket 'approved' (issued = approved to build); nothing is guessed per-record. All 68 Permit_Type values enumerated via groupBy; trade/noise permits (Garage Sale 5421, Electrical General 12460, plumbing/mechanical/reroof/fence/sign/pool etc.) are dropped at source via extra_where, mirroring the SLC paperwork-drop. No per-record link -> the official REST layer is the dataset_url fallback. title changed Project_Name -> [Permit_Type, Address] after first cache: Project_Name is frequently boilerplate ('Building No: ; Unit No: N/A') — the SLC-style concat title is always real and distinct.

arcgis.extra_where: CaseType IN ('Concept Plan','Development Agreement','Planned Development Agreement','Final Plat','Preliminary Plat','Amending Plat','Replat','Simple Amendment to Plat','Conditional Use Permit','TABC Conditional Use Permit','TABC Restricted Conditional Use Permit','Zoning Change','Land Use Amendment','Annexation','Certificate of Appropriateness','Special Exception','Variance','Municipal Utility District','Public Improvement District')
arcgis._receipts: Verified live 2026-07-13 via pg_net: city ArcGIS Server (smgis.sanmarcostx.gov), PlanningCase_Point layer 15, point geometry, fresh (created_date Jul 2026), 11 statuses + 36 case types VERBATIM from groupBy. Address has no ZIP and no ZIP column, so the constant zip_where_template scopes the whole city feed to San Marcos's principal ZIP 78666 (records still place by their OWN per-parcel geometry; blank statuses are excluded by the connector's blank->exclude rule). Paperwork/non-development types (Pre-Development Meeting 1689, Zoning Verification 343, Short Term Rental/Home-Share registrations, Release from ETJ, Out-of-City utility connections, blank 347) dropped at source via extra_where. No per-record link -> official REST layer as dataset_url fallback. recency_days=365 added after first live cache: the layer holds the full multi-year case archive (2,154 dev records on 78666) — the Austin issued-permits precedent window keeps the page a what's-changing view, not an archive.

arcgis.extra_where: Permit_Type IN ('Commercial Building Permit','Residential Building Permit','Residential Accessory Struct','Commercial Accessory Structure','Commercial Grading Permit','Design Review')
arcgis._receipts: Verified live 2026-07-13 via pg_net: City of Fort Worth's own Enterprise server (mapit.fortworthtexas.gov, CIVIC/Permits, CFW_AGOL_ADMIN org item) - a full Accela extract with point geometry, NATIVE Zip_Code column, 28 Current_Status values + 11 Permit_Type values enumerated VERBATIM via groupBy, fresh through File_Date 2026-07-12. Trade permits (Mechanical 120k, Electrical 162k, Plumbing 167k+74k backflow, Urban Forestry) dropped at source via extra_where; 6 construction/design types kept. recency_days=365 keeps a what's-changing view (the layer holds 1M+ historical rows; Finaled 562k -> bucket operating). Discovery note: the city's Socrata portal (data.fortworthtexas.gov) is decommissioned - its catalog entry for 'Development Permits quz7-xnsy' 404s; this Enterprise layer is the live successor. No per-record link -> official REST layer as dataset_url fallback.

arcgis.extra_where: CANCEL IS NULL AND CLASS IN ('New Building','Addition','New Non-Building Structure','Phased Construction','Foundation Only/Excavation','Foundation Only/ Excavation','Alteration/Tenant Finish')
arcgis._receipts: Verified live 2026-07-14 via pg_net: Denver's own AGO org (zdB7qR0BtYrg0Xpl, Open Data Catalog ODC_DEV layers) - POINT geometry (wkid 2877 -> outSR 4326), fresh through DATE_ISSUED 2026-07-09, 2,438 permits issued in the past year city-wide. NO ZIP attribute anywhere (ADDRESS carries no ZIP, LOCATION null) -> first consumer of the additive spatial_zip_radius_mi connector option: an envelope +-3 mi around the ZIP centroid (the engine's standard ZIP approximation; records keep their OWN per-parcel points). Every record IS an issued permit (dataset-level fact, san-antonio precedent) so the 7 dev-relevant CLASS values map to bucket approved; noise classes (Repair/Replace 2533, Special Event 758, Change of Contractor 493, Change of Use Only, Move, legacy numeric codes) + cancelled rows (CANCEL IS NULL) dropped at source. CLASS values are VERBATIM from a live groupBy (mixed case - the residential layer is UPPERCASE, mapped separately). No per-record link -> official REST layer as dataset_url fallback.

arcgis.extra_where: CANCEL IS NULL AND CLASS IN ('New Building','Addition','New Non-Building Structure','Phased Construction','Foundation Only/Excavation','Foundation Only/ Excavation')
arcgis._receipts: Verified live 2026-07-14 via pg_net: same Denver AGO org/layer family as the commercial entry - POINT geometry, fresh through DATE_ISSUED 2026-07-11. STANDING ANSWER learned on first re-cache: the groupBy statistics endpoint on THIS layer CASE-FOLDS values to UPPERCASE ('NEW BUILDING 33705'), but the actual stored row values are MIXED CASE ('New Building') - verified via returnDistinctValues=true (Repair/Replace | Addition | New Non-Building Structure | Phased Construction | Foundation Only/Excavation | New Building | ...). The first registration used the uppercase groupBy values, the case-sensitive status lookup excluded every fetched row (fail-closed working as designed - 0 records, nothing wrong published), and this corrected entry uses the row-verbatim mixed-case values. ALWAYS confirm casing with returnDistinctValues, never groupBy alone. Residential remodel noise (Alteration/Tenant Finish, Repair/Replace, Change of Contractor/Use, Wreck, legacy codes) dropped at source; issued-permit dataset-level fact -> included classes map to bucket approved. Spatial ZIP scoping (spatial_zip_radius_mi 3).

arcgis.extra_where: PermitType IN ('Building Permit - Single Family Detached Dwelling','Building Permit - Non-Residential','Building Permit - Accessory','Building Permit - Multifamily','Building Permit - Single Family Attached Dwelling','Building Permit - Townhome','Building Permit - Mixed Use','Demolition Permit','Grading Permit','Mobile Home Permit')
arcgis._receipts: Verified live 2026-07-14 via pg_net: City of Boulder's own AGO org (ePKBjXrBZ2vEEgWd, found via the open-data.bouldercolorado.gov DCAT) - a BLDS-style permit TABLE (no geometry): rows geocode via the full OriginalAddress+City+State (connector's address path, geocode-cache-backed) and carry a NATIVE OriginalZip. Fresh through AppliedDate 2026-07-07 (string ISO dates). 24 StatusCurrent values + 29 PermitType values enumerated VERBATIM via groupBy; trade noise (Mechanical 71549, Electrical 66045, Roofing 28236, Plumbing 28210, ROW 21952, fences/signs/events) dropped at source, keeping the 10 building/demolition/grading types. Per-ZIP active volume ~100-240 rows/yr (80301-80305) - geocoding-manageable; if the first live re-cache times out, flip zip_mode:false (Austin precedent) as the one-field fallback. No per-record public link -> REST layer dataset fallback.

arcgis.extra_where: PERMITTYPE IN ('Residential New Single Family Attached','Residential New Single Family Detached','Residential New Multi-Family','Residential New Duplex','Residential Addition','Residential New Secondary Building','Commercial New Com-Ind-Mixed-Use Building','Commercial New Secondary Building','Commercial Addition','Demolition')
arcgis._receipts: Verified live 2026-07-14 via pg_net: City of Fort Collins' own AGO org (dLpFH5mwVvxSN4OE) 'Current Building Permits' layer, found via the successor hub open-data-fcgov.hub.arcgis.com (the old opendata.fcgov.com Socrata portal is DECOMMISSIONED - 503 'This site has moved'). POINT geometry, NATIVE ZIP column, and a PER-RECORD official Accela link (WEB -> accela-aca.fcgov.com CapDetail) = record-precision record_url. A curated CURRENT-permits snapshot (2,177 rows; no date column -> no recency filter; the layer itself is the currency guarantee, Provo CurrentProjects precedent). 14 statuses + 13 types enumerated VERBATIM via groupBy; Stock Plan (647) and General Alteration remodel noise (708) dropped at source, keeping the 10 new-construction/addition/demolition types. 'Issued FF' (1 row) left unmapped on purpose (ambiguous semantics -> connector excludes + surfaces it).

Verified live 2026-07-14 via pg_net: Colorado Springs' OWN ArcGIS Server (gis.coloradosprings.gov, Planning/PlanDevTracker_PRO - 'Map Service referenced in the Web Map Item for the Development Tracker site', i.e. the city's own Development Tracker backend). POINT geometry Planning_Applications layer, 967 current land-use cases; 9 record_status + 15 record_type values enumerated VERBATIM via groupBy (all planning/land-use case types - no trade noise to drop). record_address carries NO ZIP and there is no date column -> spatial_zip_radius_mi 3 (second consumer of the additive envelope option) and no recency filter (the tracker holds only current cases; real samples: 'FRONT RANGE BARBECUE - Development Plan' DEPN-23-0205 Approved, 'Project Rockrimmon' DEPN-24-0029). No per-record public deep link derivable from record_id -> official REST layer dataset fallback.

arcgis.extra_where: PERMITTYPE IN ('BS','BR','BB','BA','BM','BW','BZ','BY','BE','BC','BJ','BV','BH','GD','GJ','GH','GA','GE','LL','LG','LN','LF','LU','LK','LP','LQ','LB','LA','LD','LI','LW','WG','AC','LE','LX')

No per-record link column -> dataset_url precision.

arcgis.extra_where: permit_type IN ('Building','Land Use','Site')
arcgis._receipts: 2026-07-15: max application_date 2026-07-13 (item modified 2026-07-14); 80 statuses + 10 permit_type values VERBATIM via returnDistinctValues; native zip (98402=8,359 all-time -> 365d window); per-record Accela link column. Noise types (ePermit trades, Sign, Special Event, ROW, Utility Connection, Historic, Pre-Application) dropped at source. Found via org-scoped AGO search (orgid:SCwJH1pD8WSn5T5y) - the geohub DCAT path serves HTML, and an UNSCOPED sharing/rest/search returns Calgary lookalikes.

arcgis.extra_where: Application_Type <> 'Sex-Oriented Business' AND Application_Received >= '2025/07/01'
arcgis._receipts: 2026-07-16 CA wire pass. Anaheim's own AGO org (services3/hPs600I3X0RTaaaq, service list enumerated live). GEOMETRY-LESS TABLE (geometryType null) -> rows geocode via Location_Primary_Address (Boulder precedent; v20 geofence applies). No ZIP column; every address embeds '..., Anaheim, Ca 92xxx' -> zip_where_template LIKE (live receipt: 92805 -> 6,333 rows all-time). FRESH: newest Application_Received 2026/06/30 (PAZ2026-00384, matches recon). 26,883 rows total / 845 since 2025-07-01 -> recency via extra_where STRING compare (dates are yyyy/mm/dd STRINGS; the connector's recency_days DATE literal would fail on a string field - do NOT set it). 23 statuses VERBATIM via returnDistinctValues 2026-07-16: 'Modified' left unmapped ON PURPOSE (ambiguous outcome - surfaces in run report); Closed/Complete excluded (MN 'Closed' precedent: no trustworthy lifecycle). 'Sex-Oriented Business' type dropped at source (licensing class, not development). record_url dataset-precision (no per-record public link; CityView-class portals are login-walled - Provo precedent).

arcgis._receipts: 2026-07-16 AZ wire pass. FIRST consumer of the additive arcgis `spatial_latlng_cols` option (attribute-bbox: a geometry-less hosted TABLE whose rows carry per-record Latitude/Longitude COLUMNS - the Detroit-tables cousin; a geometry envelope param is meaningless on a table, so the envelope is AND'd into WHERE; offline unit-tested incl. envelope-mode regression). FRESH: newest IssueDate 2026-07-10 (#324234, live receipt; esriFieldTypeDate -> recency_days DATE literal valid). 7 PermitStatus values VERBATIM (REFUND/EXPIRED/ACTIVE/FINALLED/PENDING/WITHDRAWN/ON HOLD; nulls fail closed): ACTIVE->approved, FINALLED->operating, PENDING+ON HOLD->proposed, rest excluded. ~190 PermitType values enumerated live; kept 70 construction/development classes verbatim (SFR new/addition/remodel, multi-family, commercial new/shell/addition, hotels, churches/schools/care, demolition, foundation-only, guest houses); DROPPED at source: tenant improvements, signs, pools/spas, fences/walls, patio covers/carports, solar, water heaters, minimum-charge trade permits, special events, native-plant/NAOS paperwork. NOTE 'APARTMENTS GREEN' carries trailing whitespace at source (Bellevue-class variant) and is NOT in the keep list - rows of that class surface as excluded-by-extra_where, never guessed. Types /13 Cases + /15 COO same shape (not wired: COO=occupancy noise; Cases=queue). No per-row URL column -> dataset-precision record_url.

Permit - Alteration/Addition','Comm. Permit - Razing','Res. Permit - Razing','Comm. Permit - Grading','Res. Permit - Grading','Comm. Permit - Foundation Only','Res. Permit - Accessory Structure')
arcgis._receipts: 2026-07-16 MD wire pass. The county's own ArcGIS Server (bcgisdata, DevelopmentManagement/ActiveDevelopment layer 4 - the Hub distribution host, NOT the hub domain). FRESH: newest ISSDATE 2026-07-15 (epoch 1784073600000; R24-07845/R26-03840 live receipt; esriFieldTypeDate -> recency_days valid). 6 STATUS values VERBATIM (returnDistinctValues): ISSUE->approved, OPEN->proposed, CLOSED->operating, EXPIRED/CANCELLED/BL-EXPIRED->exclude. DESCRIPTION_TYPE vocab VERBATIM ('Comm. Permit - X'/'Res. Permit - X' format - the recon note paraphrased; the IN-list here is byte-exact from the live enumeration): kept 11 construction/land-use classes; DROPPED at source: Sign, Swimming Pool, Deck, Fence, Solar, Tanks, Fireplace, COO, Sprinkler, Towers/Antennas, Temporary*, Retaining Wall, Bulkhead, Piers, Moving, Storm Water, Cranes, Private Bridge, Access Point + Env Health variants (restaurant-scoped). Native ZIP + per-record LATITUDE/LONGITUDE columns; recency on APPL_DATE keeps OPEN applications (ISSDATE null pre-issuance) - wait, file_date is APPL_DATE for exactly that reason. No per-row URL column -> dataset-precision record_url. Hub layers /5 Electrical /8 U&O dropped (trades/occupancy).

csv._receipts: 2026-07-16 CA wire pass (FIRST csv entry - new additive sources/csv.ts, fetch-once memo). FRESH SAME-DAY: max APPROVAL_ISSUE_DATE 2026-07-15 over 28,515 rows (15.0 MB YTD-2026 file; runner csv_stats receipt, run 29508593119). Vocabulary VERBATIM from the same run: statuses Issued/Closed/Inspecting/Inspection Followup/Cancelled -> Issued+Inspecting+Inspection Followup=approved, Closed=operating (Boston precedent), Cancelled=exclude; 151 type|status combos enumerated - kept 10 construction/land-use types, DROPPED at parse: Traffic Control (5,807), No-Plan trades combos, Photovoltaic SB 379 (rooftop solar), Construction Noise, Transportation, Right Of Way Pmt-Const Plan, Electrical/Mechanical/Plumbing Pmt, Fire Pmt variants, Sign Pmt, Zone History Letter + paperwork classes. NO ZIP column -> spatial_zip_radius_mi 3 on each row's OWN GIS_LATITUDE/GIS_LONGITUDE (which also self-excludes the file's garbage-coordinate rows: max GIS_LONGITUDE=324108.6 observed - a bad point can never sit near a centroid). RECORD-PRECISION record_url VERIFIED: opendsd.sandiego.gov API discriminates (ApprovalId 2618042 -> full record matching the CSV row; 999999999 -> 'could not be found') so /web/approvals/{APPROVAL_ID} is a real per-record official page. PROJECT_STATUS NOT used (17,813 blanks); APPROVAL_STATUS is the status column. File is YTD-2026 (inherent recency window).

carto.extra_where: permittype IN ('Building','Residential Building','Demolition','Zoning') AND typeofwork IN ('Addition and/or Alteration','Change of Use','New construction, addition, GFA change','New Construction','New Construction (Shell Only)','City Demolition','Full Demolition','Minor Demolition','Major Demolition','Lot Line Relocation','Combined Lot Line Relocation and New Development','Conditional Zoning Approval')
carto._receipts: 2026-07-16 PA wire pass (FIRST carto entry - new additive sources/carto.ts, SQL API; 16 offline unit checks incl. bidirectional gate proof). FRESH: max permitissuedate 2026-07-10 over the city's official L&I permits table (live SQL receipt). Statuses VERBATIM within the kept types over 365d: Issued 9,302 / Completed 2,611 / Amendment-* lifecycle -> proposed / Expired+Cancelled+Amendment Denied+Withdrawn (+ Stop Work/Refused/Denied from the full-table + sample receipts) excluded. typeofwork vocab VERBATIM: kept 12 construction/land-use values (Addition and/or Alteration 5,007, Change of Use 2,406, New construction+GFA 1,877, New Construction 971, demolition classes, lot-line actions); DROPPED at source: Make Safe permits (safety orders), Signs, Parking Only, Family Daycare, Fences Only, Site Clearing + the Plumbing 13,300/Electrical 8,187/Mechanical/Fire Suppression trade classes via the permittype IN-list. ZIP is ZIP+4 ('19143-3005') -> the connector's LIKE prefix scoping + 5-digit truncation. Geometry via PostGIS ST_Y/ST_X(the_geom) - live-verified real Philly coords. record_url dataset-precision on the SQL endpoint (OpenDataPhilly portal 404s - retired; no per-record public URL pattern verified; the machine endpoint IS the official record interface - Boulder precedent). NOTE: recency on permitissuedate excludes null-issue-date zoning applications (they carry Refused/Denied statuses in the sample - excluded anyway).
